supervisor.rationale: Phase is `review` with `pre_pr_review` and `codex_reviewer` unsatisfied; `deep-review` is the team-based PR review that satisfies `pre_pr_review` and is the canonical next step before shipping (codex review can follow).

findings.title: ReviewReport.summary max_length=500 constraint silently removed
findings.body: The Pydantic constraint `Field(max_length=500)` on `ReviewReport.summary` was removed without a CHANGELOG entry or inline comment. ReviewReport is the core contract returned by every code-review worker and is consumed by aggregators, ledger storage, and the V3 dogfood artifact — removing the bound changes the implicit invariant downstream consumers may rely on (display truncation, storage costs, prompt cost when summaries are re-fed). If the change exists to accommodate the dogfood worker's longer summary, that rationale belongs either in a comment or in the v4.6.0 'Changed' section.
findings.suggested_fix: Either restore `max_length=500` and have workers/aggregators truncate at the boundary, or keep the relaxation but document it in CHANGELOG.md under [4.6.0] Changed and add a short inline comment on the field explaining why the bound was dropped.

findings.title: Dirty marker '*' committed into git_hash YAML value
findings.body: The git_hash frontmatter field holds `3a3f257*` — the trailing `*` is the transient 'locally modified' display marker emitted by `update-plugin-versions.sh`, not a value that should be persisted. CHANGELOG acknowledges this as a known limitation of the version updater, but the documented workaround (commit pending edits, then re-run the updater) wasn't applied here. Every other git_hash in this diff is a clean 7-hex value. V3 dogfood independently flagged this same file/line.
findings.suggested_fix: Stage and commit pending edits to this file, then re-run `./update-plugin-versions.sh` so the field becomes a clean hex value. Alternatively, strip the trailing `*` in this commit and address the underlying updater bug in a follow-up.

findings.title: Staging hint may over-stage untracked files under plugin tree
findings.body: The remediation hint was widened from `git add plugins/requirements-framework/agents/*.md` to `git add plugins/requirements-framework/` (no `-u`). This correctly catches newly-rendered `.md` siblings of brand-new `.md.j2` files, but it also stages any other untracked file under the plugin tree (work-in-progress drafts, scratch files). The accompanying comment notes the intent, but a user following the hint blindly could sneak unintended files into a commit. V3 dogfood independently flagged this same trade-off.
findings.suggested_fix: Either leave the broad hint but add a one-line follow-up suggesting `git status` review before committing, or split into two explicit globs: `git add -u plugins/requirements-framework/ && git add plugins/requirements-framework/**/*.md plugins/requirements-framework/**/*.md.j2`.

report.summary: Step 16c conversion is well-executed: byte-identical .md.j2 sources guarded by an MD5 gate plus a new regression test (test_all_plugin_md_files_have_j2_source). The case-sensitivity fix in update-plugin-versions.sh (skill.md → SKILL.md) repairs a silent zero-match bug worth shipping. Three issues are worth flagging: a dirty-marker '*' was committed into a git_hash YAML value (already a known-limitation per CHANGELOG, but the workaround wasn't applied here); the ReviewReport.summary `max_length=500` Pydantic constraint was silently removed with no CHANGELOG entry or rationale; and the pre-commit-check.sh staging hint now over-stages untracked files under plugins/requirements-framework/.

aggregator.skipped_reason: single worker; aggregator is moot for N=1 reports

langfuse.note: Open Langfuse UI to see auto-instrumented spans (supervisor + worker, each a top-level trace).
